steps.title: Spot the key contrast: impulse vs. calculated planning
steps.content: The phrase “pose so subtly considered that if one were not on the lookout one mistook it for impulse” reveals the decisive signal: Osmond's actions appear spontaneous but are actually the result of careful deliberation. This shows that “consideration” refers to calculated thinking.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
The word "consideration" needs to mean deliberate, premeditated thinking — Osmond's poses are so carefully planned they appear spontaneous. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Deference: Ralph lived so much in the land of deference — deference means submission or yielding to another's wishes, but Osmond is exercising self-controlled scheming, not submitting to anyone. ✗
  • Choice B — Calculation: Ralph lived so much in the land of calculation — calculation means deliberate, premeditated planning, which perfectly matches poses "subtly considered" to look like impulse. ✓
  • Choice C — Indecision: Ralph lived so much in the land of indecision — indecision means uncertainty or hesitation, but Osmond's poses are purposeful and controlled, the opposite of hesitant. ✗
  • Choice D — Courtesy: Ralph lived so much in the land of courtesy — courtesy means polite behavior, which has nothing to do with Osmond's calculated, purposeful posturing. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
